rulings.Case Title: RICHARD ESCOBAR VS. ENVISION GENERAL CONTRACTING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RICARDO ESCOBAR's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. VS. GRANT LOMBARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing Of Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A.'s Hearing on Judgment Debtor's Claim Of Exemption. Judgment Debtor Lombard's claim of exemption is denied. Judgment Debtor has not carried his burden of showing his earnings are exempt. (CCP § 703.580.) Further, Lombard has not met his burden of tracing his exempt funds into the levied account. (CCP § 703.080.) The San Francisco County Sheriff's Office is ordered to release the $951.32 it is currently holding to Judgment Creditor towards satisfaction of the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SF BAY MARINAS FOR ALL, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 6, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST PETE'S ENTERPRISES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action as against the real party in interest. The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 are sustained without leave to amend. At paragraph 32 of the complaint/petition, plaintiff concedes that the demolition of the harbor is complete. Accordingly, those claims are moot and plaintiff fails to highlight any germane exception to the mootness doctrine. See Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559. Plaintiff cannot allege causes of action 4 and 5 against real party in interest because plaintiff pleads no facts showing that real party is a state actor. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 6 without leave to amend. An injunction is not a distinct cause of action and causes of action 4 and 5 seek injunctive relief. See Shell Oil Co. v. Marinas for All (1942) 52 Cal.App.2d 164. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SF BAY MARINAS FOR ALL, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 6, RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 are sustained without leave to amend. At paragraph 32 of the complaint/petition, plaintiff concedes that the demolition of the harbor is complete. Accordingly, those claims are moot and plaintiff fails to highlight any germane exception to the mootness doctrine. See Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559. The demurrers to causes of action 4 and 5 are overruled. Plaintiff can maintain the claims based on the federal constitution and 42 USC 1983. It is premature to determine whether defendant is entitled to 11th Amendment immunity as an arm of the state. See Lynch v. SFHA (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 527. The Court denies defendant's request for judicial notice under Evid. 452(c) and the attached document does not clearly show that defendant is an arm of the state. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 6 without leave to amend. An injunction is not a distinct cause of action and causes of action 4 and 5 seek injunctive relief. See Shell Oil Co. v. Marinas for All (1942) 52 Cal.App.2d 164. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ABDOL NOVIN, AS TRUSTEE OF THE NOVIN FAMILY TRUST VS. THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Pltff To Provide Further Responses To Csc'S Special Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT CSC, INC., DBA CONSTRUCTION SERVICES COMPANY's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Further Responses To CSC's Special Interrogatories (Set No. 1) And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The motions are timely. Defendant sent a letter on October 21, 2013 (Ex. T) confirming a phone call with plaintiff by which it was agreed that he would provide further discovery responses. The letter referred back to a May 8, 2013 meet and confer letter which addressed a wide variety of discovery--not just document production. There are no documents or declarations evidencing plaintiff's disagreement with the confirming letter. The parties shall meet and confer before the hearing to attempt to narrow or fully resolve the discovery disputes. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MCALEESE, PAUL (DBA 530 JACKSON STREET, INC.) VS. RICHARD T. FERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTs RICHARD FERKO and RICHARD T. FERKO, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to individual Plaintiff McAleese. Plaintiff McAleese has failed to allege facts that he is the real party of interest to sue on behalf of corporate Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street. (See Fladeboe v. American Isuzu Motors Inc. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 42, 55.) Defendants' demurrer to all causes of action on the grounds that they are barred under the applicable statute of limitations is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that it did not discover the alleged wrongful conduct until May 2012 (SAC 14), and the original complaint was filed on February 14, 2013. The four-year provision under CCP § 340.6(a) is tolled because Defendant continued to represent Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street regarding the underlying litigation. (CCP 340.6(a); SAC 10-19.) Defendants' demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action is overruled as Plaintiff has alleged facts with sufficient particularity to meet the heightened pleading standard of fraud. Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action for accounting is overruled as Plaintiffs have alleged facts that a fiduciary relationship exits and that Plaintiff never received an accounting of the settlement money that Defendants collected. (SAC 10, 13, 36.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION, VS. YOLANDA'S CONSTRUCTION ADMINISTRATION & TRAFFIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD

rulings.Case Title: HELEN MICKIEWICZ VS. OPERA PLAZA MASTERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Second Mtn And Second Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF HELEN MICKIEWICZ's Second Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. The parties shall come prepared to submit evidence as to whether form interrogatories were served by plaintiff, and, if so, what the responses were. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PADWAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MICHAEL VS. NAPOLI, BERN,RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And Dba Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT NAPOLI BERN RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLP's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Privilege Objections As To Michael Padway, Individually And Dba Michael Padway & Associates' Requests For Production Of Documents And From Order Compelling Production Of Confidential Attorney-Client Communications And Award Of Sanctions. To be heard before Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne on February 24, 2014 at 9:00 am in Department 302. Although titled a "Motion for Relief from Waiver of Privilege Objections," Defendants move for reconsideration of an order issued by Pro Tem Judge Renne on January 6, 2014. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, was assigned to hear the underlying motion. On January 6, 2014, the parties stipulated to Pro Tem Judge Renne hearing the underlying motion that is the subject of reconsideration. The parties are deemed to have stipulated to the authority of Pro Tem Judge Renne for the instant motion for reconsideration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)
